title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the instrumented arena and object pool contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for instrumented arena and object pool after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Arena Bump Invariant · changed case · Review Arena Bump Invariant

review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 4627. Produce bounded C evidence to maintain checked arena offset and high-water invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant.
